_comment: The Home World — tightened. A compact island where every region is pulled close to a single curving SPINE (the 'finding path'): you wake alone in the grove (east), pass indifferent pals, reach the one that is yours, gather at the always-lit Commons fire, and end at the Threshold of portals (west). The Overlook, Hollow and Meadow hang just off the spine; still water lines the southern edge. The footprint is deliberately small so density stays high at low population. Coordinates are world-space pixels. Colors are [r,g,b] 0..1. world_art draws it; world_controller uses 'props'/'portals' for proximity. Each prop/portal's 'type' picks how it's drawn (see world_art). Edit freely to reshape the place.
_regions_comment: Named AREAS for new-area discovery (a bond source). The companion's bond deepens the first time it reaches each region (novelty-gated, so re-entering pays nothing). An area's id is world_id:region_id; a position in no region is just the world_id (the open 'wilds' — here, the intentional hush between beats). Because ids are world-namespaced and the companion remembers across sessions/worlds, this scales from sub-regions of one big world to entirely separate worlds. Rectangles in world space; first match wins; gaps fall through to the world default area.
_border_comment: A procedural, jittered treeline just inside the bounds so the world feels enclosed rather than ending at the void — here it reads as the drifting mist where the world softens and ends. Presentation-only (world_art builds it); seeded, so it's identical every run.
_collision_comment: Barriers. tree_radius/great_tree_radius are the blocking circles for trees and landmark great-trees; pond_blocks makes water solid (the southern still water is the world's edge); body_radius is how big the player/companion are for collision; margin stops them a touch short of things. Tall props (bench, signpost, lantern, crystal, log, berry_bush) block by default — any prop can override with its own "solid": true/false and "collision_radius".
_landmarks_comment: Oversized, beckoning features (a great tree) you can spot from afar — anchors that pull you to explore. Presentation-only; drawn by world_art._draw_landmark.
_atmosphere_comment: Presentation-only mood knobs — none of this touches game logic. Tuned toward DUSK so the world reads dim and the warm centres (the Commons fire, the finding, the portals) glow against it — the whole point of the tightened footprint. day_tint is a global color-wash (CanvasModulate); vignette darkens the screen edges; wind drives the gentle sway of flowers/canopies/grass; ground_noise mottles the flat ground; pollen is the count of drifting motes (read here as mist) that follow the camera; glow.pulse_speed breathes the lantern/crystal/portal light.
_water_comment: Still water along the southern edge only — one clean shore the meadow opens onto. Circular ponds (pond_blocks true) so it reads still, not flowing.
_paths_comment: The SPINE (grove -> pals -> finding -> Commons -> Threshold) plus three short trails out to the regions that hang off it (Overlook, Hollow, Meadow). Straight segments chained beat to beat.
_props_comment: Each has a stable 'id' — the companion keys its habituation/memory off it, so editing a prop's position or label keeps its identity (and the bond it earned). Keep ids unique and stable across edits. 'tags' are NEUTRAL descriptors of the thing; the companion's appraisal (see companion.json 'appraisal') decides how it feels about them — author tags freely without thinking about psychology. A prop is examinable only if it opts in with 'interactive': true. Everything else is STATIC SCENERY: world_art still draws it (by 'type') and it still blocks movement, but it's not examinable and the companion isn't drawn to it — it conveys what it is by appearance alone. Here each prop dresses one beat: the signpost/berry bush mark the grove where you wake, the chime stone sits among the pals, the crystal is the finding, the lantern is the always-lit Commons fire, the bench is the Overlook seat, the log rests in the Hollow, the wildflowers/basin line the Meadow shore.
_portals_comment: Doorways to other worlds — walk through one to travel. world_controller renders them (via world_art's 'portal' type) and watches for the player stepping into them; WorldRouter performs the scene swap and the arriving world sets you down beside its matching 'target_portal'. The four portal ids here (vale_portal/vale_bazaar_portal/vale_ruin_portal/vale_maze_portal) are the arrival anchors the other worlds point back to — keep them stable. All four are clustered at the western Threshold.

portals.label: a gap in a tall hedge

_ambient_pals_comment: Server-authoritative AMBIENT PALS — set-dressing creatures (the SVG's 'ambient pals — not yours') that wander so the world reads as alive even when few players are online. Server.AmbientPals simulates each within home ± roam_radius and broadcasts its position ~10 Hz; the client spawns one brainless companion puppet per id and eases it. NOT yours, no bonding, no interaction. 'home' is the wander anchor (and initial spot); 'roam_radius' how far it strays. The server steers pals around solids (trees, great-trees, tall props, ponds) and starts one clear of any it's placed on, so disks no longer need to be hand-cleared — though a large obstacle still walls off part of a disk. 'look' is the resting-look (ear/bounce/tail/gaze/coat/size). Clustered at the pals, Hollow and Meadow beats per the map.
